items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Price LIVE: Indian markets traded higher in early deals but sentiment stayed cautious amid US-Iran ceasefire uncertainty, with volatility likely as investors track crude oil prices, global cues, Q4 earnings and weekly derivatives expiry.

items.title: Maharashtra considers rationing of fertilisers, says minister Dattatray Bharane
items.summary: Maharashtra will ration fertiliser sales to farmers for the upcoming kharif season. The state government seeks Centre approval for this plan. This move aims to prevent malpractices and ensure fair distribution. Digital data on sowing and landholdings will help estimate farmer needs.

items.title: Gold, silver price today, 21 April: Check rates of 24 kt, 22 kt gold and 999 silver in Delhi, Mumbai, other cities
items.summary: Gold and silver prices remain volatile, driven by a stronger US dollar amid rising crude oil prices. As global oil trade is dollar-denominated, higher oil prices boost demand for the greenback, putting pressure on gold prices.

items.title: Quality and branding: The twin pillars of India’s global horticulture push
items.summary: A coordinated push involving policy support, private investment, and technology adoption can help India shift from volume-driven to value-led horticulture by fixing productivity, post-harvest, and market gaps, say experts.

items.title: In charts: How mutual fund investors responded to the March volatility
items.summary: Despite war-driven volatility and a March market tumble, Indian investors stayed the course, favouring equity schemes and record SIP contributions over panic selling—even as SIP stoppages outpaced fresh registrations during the month.

items.title: MFs raise stakes in ICICI Bank as FPIs trim exposure in FY26
items.summary: Mutual fund holdings climbed to 32.42% as of March 31, 2026, up from 29.86% a year earlier

items.title: Rupee falls 16 paise to 93.32 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: The rupee fell 16 paise to 93.32 against the US dollar in early trade as the American currency strengthened and the Reserve Bank eased speculative betting restrictions. Positive domestic equity markets and foreign fund inflows offered some support, despite West Asia peace agreement uncertainties.

items.title: South Korea's Kospi hits record high amid mixed Asia markets as hopes linger for Mideast peace
items.summary: Korea's Kospi hits fresh intraday high, amid mixed Asian markets as investors brushed aside fresh threats from Trump on Iran ahead of a fragile ceasefire deadline.

items.title: SpaceX tries to woo Wall Street with three-day analyst meeting this week, sources say
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for a historic Initial Public Offering. The company is hosting analysts this week for briefings at its Texas launch facility and Tennessee data center. SpaceX aims to raise $75 billion in what could be the world's largest IPO. A trading debut is targeted for late June. Retail investors will also be allocated a significant portion of shares.

items.title: Adani Enterprises sets board meeting date to declare Q4 results, dividend
items.summary: Adani Enterprises will discuss dividends and fundraising at the upcoming board meeting, along with Q4 results 2026. Despite weak near-term stock performance, the company has delivered strong long-term gains.

items.title: ‘New cards on the battlefield’: U.S., Iran ratchet up rhetoric with peace talks in limbo
items.summary: American negotiators may be outmatched at the negotiating table by Iran's experienced diplomatic delegation, says analyst, ahead of potential high-stakes meeting.

items.title: Is the Indian middle class dream over? Saurabh Mukherjea on the 3 forces crushing your future
items.summary: India's middle class is in deep trouble. Education no longer guarantees prosperity. Jobs are disappearing, especially in IT. Wages are not keeping pace with rising costs. Household debt is soaring, often for consumption. AI is further threatening jobs. This consumption engine slowdown impacts the economy. The middle class has limited political voice.

items.title: Clock ticking on Tuesday deadline: How will markets react if Iran ceasefire breaks or holds?
items.summary: Indian markets brace for a crucial Tuesday deadline as a ceasefire between Iran, the US, and Israel nears expiration. The direction of crude oil prices, influenced by potential de-escalation or renewed hostilities, will likely dictate the trajectory of equities, currencies, and bond yields. Investors are closely monitoring oil's reaction to geopolitical developments.

items.title: Nvidia supplier Victory Giant sees shares soar 60% in Hong Kong debut
items.summary: Shares of Victory Giant Technology surged in what is Hong Kong's largest initial public offering debut so far this year.

items.title: Anil Ambani-owned Reliance Power shares jump, rise 40% in April. Trap or an opportunity before Q4 results 2026?
items.summary: Anil Ambani-owned Reliance Power share price has risen from ₹20.36 to ₹28.60 apiece in April 2026

items.title: Silver slips Rs 2,300, Gold at Rs 1.53 lakh as investors await U.S.-Iran peace talks this weekend; key levels to watch
items.summary: Gold and silver prices edged lower on MCX amid a stronger dollar, with investors tracking potential U.S.-Iran talks. Analysts expect continued volatility driven by oil prices and currency moves. Key support and resistance levels suggest a buy-on-dips strategy, while physical gold prices remain steady across major Indian cities.

items.title: Markets open higher as Iran ceasefire hopes ease crude fears; Shriram Finance, Adani Ports lead gains
items.summary: The morning rally was underpinned by reports that Iran will send a delegation for a second round of talks with the United States, with fresh negotiations expected in Pakistan this week

items.title: US Stock Market: Johny Srouji to lead unified hardware team as Apple prepares for leadership transition
items.summary: Apple is restructuring its hardware organisation by merging the engineering and technologies divisions into a unified leadership model. The company will split operations into five core areas, aiming to streamline development across key products. The move also signals major leadership changes, with expanded roles for senior executives and a clearer succession path.

items.title: El Niño and geopolitical tensions cloud India’s rural economy: A somber 2026 outlook
items.summary: India’s rural economy faces pressure in 2026 from a likely El Niño-driven weak monsoon and rising input costs due to US-Iran tensions. Lower farm output, weak incomes, and elevated inflation may hit consumption, while higher fertiliser subsidies could strain fiscal balances, keeping the overall outlook cautious.

items.title: Crude oil futures trade lower on US-Iran talks report
items.summary: At 9:19 am on Tuesday, June Brent oil futures were $95.01, down 0.49%, and June WTI crude oil futures were $86.65, down 0.88%

items.summary: Vedanta's share price increased by over 3% to hit its 52-week high following the announcement of May 1 as the record date for its business demerger into four independent companies. Shareholders will receive shares in a 1:1 ratio.

items.summary: Oil prices slipped over 1% on Tuesday amid hopes of peace talks between the US and Iran. Here's what experts say on oil prices' near-term outlook.

items.title: Garment exporters see demand recovering as US tariff pressures ease
items.summary: Indian garment exporters expect a strong business rebound in the second half of the fiscal year. Easing US tariff pressures are bringing buyers back. Orders for the first two quarters were booked at peak tariffs. Revenue improvement will be visible from October. Exporters are diversifying markets to Europe and Africa.

The company announced a stunning 19 percent increase in net profit compared to last year, prompting brokerages like Morgan Stanley and JM Financial to reaffirm their buy recommendations.

Adani Ports, ICICI Bank, and L&T were among the top gainers. Realty stocks also performed well. IT stocks experienced minor declines.

Despite geopolitical tensions, market sentiment has shifted to a 'buy-on-dips' approach, with experts suggesting the market is near a bottom. Analysts advise staggered buying as near-term direction depends on Middle East de-escalation, crude oil prices, and foreign flows.

items.title: Gold price today: Rate drops on MCX due to profit booking; lingering uncertainty on US-Iran talks weighs on sentiment
items.summary: Gold and silver rates have been highly volatile of late, largely due to the rise in the US dollar driven by a spike in crude oil prices. When oil prices rise, demand for the dollar rises, as oil is largely traded in US dollars. This drives the greenback higher, weighing on gold prices.

Experts suggest the rupee's future depends on oil prices and geopolitical events, with expectations of trading between 92.65 and 93.45.

items.title: The Golden Thumb Rule | In small caps, fundamentals—not narratives—drive returns: Aniruddha Naha
items.summary: Small-cap investing demands discipline, focusing on fundamentals like earnings and cash flows for sustainable wealth. Aniruddha Naha, CIO-Alternates, PGIM India, emphasizes that strong balance sheets and management are key. He advises investing when small caps are beaten down, not during euphoria. This approach unlocks alpha generation through discovery and re-rating over the long term.

Current market corrections offer opportunities for long-term investors, with Financials, Industrials, and Healthcare identified as key sectors poised for growth.

items.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today - 21 April 2026
items.summary: The Indian stock market ended Monday's session with nominal gains. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.

items.title: Government to propose electricity price changes in clean power push
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

items.title: Stocks to trade: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 21 April
items.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 21 April. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.

items.title: Stock recommendations for 21 April from MarketSmith India
items.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 21 April. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Tata turmoil: A snapshot of simmering tensions at the group
items.summary: Internal rifts, legal challenges, and a debate over going public have sparked a power struggle within the group following the death of Ratan Tata.

items.title: Big conglomerates’ grip on market cap steadily loosens as new-age firms rise
items.summary: A Mint analysis of 10 leading business groups by market capitalisation shows their combined share in India’s total market value has steadily declined over the past few years—from 31.4% in FY22 to 25.3% in FY25 and further to 24% in FY26.

items.title: Champagne raises a toast to growth in India, duty cuts to add to the fizz
items.summary: Under the proposed India-EU free trade agreement, duties on champagne are set to fall from 150% to 20% over seven years.

items.title: After years of distress, Kota's Resonance nears debt deal with 80% haircut
items.summary: Following the covid disruption, Kota’s coaching ecosystem experienced a prolonged period of closures and uncertainty. While legacy players such as Resonance attempted to adapt through online classes, competition intensified from new-age edtech entrants

items.title: Pocket FM's microtransactions, AI use in content creation are driving growth, COO Gangwar says
items.summary: Pocket FM's innovative approach to audio content through microtransactions and AI integration has led to remarkable growth. The platform's ability to retain users and compete in a crowded market showcases its potential for future expansion.

items.title: How Amazon allegedly used Levi's and Hanes to force rivals to raise prices - What California court filing reveals
items.summary: California's attorney general says price fixing by Amazon is caught 'explicitly and egregiously in writing.' Read what the newly unsealed court documents reveal about how it worked.

items.title: Jio Financial to leverage marketplace for high-risk products, credit cards
items.summary: Jio Financial Services Ltd chief executive officer Hitesh Sethia said the company plans to use its newly-launched marketplace to sell products that are otherwise not within its risk appetite.

items.title: South Korea Inc meets Modi; Cos including Samsung & LG looking to expand in key market, export more
items.summary: Top South Korean business leaders met Prime Minister Narendra Modi. They discussed expanding their operations in India. Companies like Samsung, LG, and Hyundai signaled fresh investments. SK Hynix is considering a chip facility. Hyundai will develop electric mobility solutions for India. This marks a significant step in bilateral economic cooperation.

items.title: Who is John Ternus? Net worth of Apple's hardware chief set to replace Tim Cook as CEO
items.summary: John Ternus, Apple's 50-year-old head of hardware engineering, will become the technology giant's chief executive officer on 1 September 2026, the company announced on Monday. Cook, 65, will move into the newly defined role of executive chairman

items.title: IPL teams appeal for tax rematch: 'Treat event as sport, not entertainment'
items.summary: Indian Premier League teams are asking the government to reclassify match tickets. They want IPL games treated as sports, not entertainment. This could cut the goods and services tax on tickets significantly. The current 40% tax is seen as too high for sporting events. Teams believe this change will benefit the sports ecosystem and its growth.

items.title: The 'dumb machine' promising a clean energy breakthrough
items.summary: A stellarator is difficult to build, but could it be the best way to make fusion energy work?

items.title: The 20-somethings juggling three jobs to make ends meet
items.summary: While UK unemployment is at a five-year-high, increasing numbers of those in work have more than one job.

items.title: Petrol thefts surge as Iran war pushes up fuel costs
items.summary: One petrol retailer says he is experiencing about five drive-offs a week at each forecourt, costing him thousands.

items.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: China ships more humanoid robots than the U.S. as investors diverge on AI bets
items.summary: Chinese startups are churning out more humanoid robots than their U.S. rivals, despite far lower valuations.

items.title: Apple names new chief executive to replace Tim Cook
items.summary: John Ternus will take over running the technology giant as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Next Round of US-Iran Peace Talks
items.summary: Gold steadied, as traders weighed prospects for a negotiated settlement to the war in Iran that’s disrupted global energy supplies and heightened inflation risks.

items.title: Apple taps John Ternus as CEO to replace Tim Cook, who will become chairman
items.summary: Apple said on Monday that John Ternus is succeeding Tim Cook as CEO, with Cook assuming the role of executive chairman on Sept. 1.

items.title: Amazon to invest up to another $25 billion in Anthropic as part of AI infrastructure deal
items.summary: Amazon is boosting its investment in Anthropic, which is committing to spending over $100 billion on Amazon cloud services over the next decade.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after nearly 15 years; John Ternus named successor
items.summary: Cook, who took over after Steve Jobs in 2011, will formally hand over the CEO role on September 1.

items.title: Apple CEO Cook is lauded by investors as he prepares for new role
items.summary: APPLECOOK-USA-STOCKSNEWS:Apple CEO Cook is lauded by investors as he prepares for new role

items.title: European Stocks Drop on Worries Over Iran Ceasefire Extension
items.summary: European stocks fell as hopes for an end to the Iran war were dashed by President Donald Trump’s comments that a ceasefire extension was “highly unlikely.”

items.title: Most Emerging Currencies Slip as Middle East Uncertainty Lingers
items.summary: Most emerging-market currencies weakened Monday after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again and President Donald Trump sent mixed signals about progress on a peace deal with the Islamic Republic.

items.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
items.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

items.title: AST SpaceMobile shares drop after its satellite is placed in wrong orbit by Bezos' Blue Origin
items.summary: Blue Origin acknowledged the satellite was placed in the wrong orbit, but has not commented since it was deemed lost.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
items.summary: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

items.title: Two Southwest Airlines planes came dangerously close in Nashville and had to take evasive action
items.summary: Two Southwest Airlines planes came dangerously close in Nashville and had to take evasive action

items.title: US dollar drops as traders remain optimistic on Iran peace breakthrough
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX:US dollar drops as traders remain optimistic on Iran peace breakthrough

items.title: 'Grid bottlenecks, execution risks slow power expansion': Report
items.summary: India's renewable energy goals are facing hurdles. Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd, or PGCIL, is struggling with delays in building power transmission lines. These delays are caused by land acquisition and other issues. This is affecting PGCIL's profits and its stock market performance. The company is undertaking a large capital expenditure program to address these issues.

items.title: SFIO widens probe into IndusInd Bank; summons audit firms over ₹2,000 crore derivatives irregularities
items.summary: The Serious Fraud Investigation Office is investigating IndusInd Bank's derivatives portfolio. Major audit firms associated with the bank over the last decade have been summoned. This probe follows questioning of former bank officials. The irregularities are estimated to have caused losses of around ₹2,000 crore. The investigation aims to uncover corporate governance issues and accounting discrepancies.

items.title: Tariff refunds begin on Monday. These retailers are due big paydays
items.summary: U.S. importers, such as Walmart and Target, could be in line for billions in tariff refunds as the government launches its claims-filing portal on Monday.

items.title: CBM licensing round draws few bidders
items.summary: The latest coal bed methane (CBM) licensing round saw a disappointing turnout, with seven of thirteen blocks receiving no bids and four attracting only a single bidder. Only two blocks will experience competitive bidding, as just three companies participated in the Special CBM Bid Round 2026, despite more liberal terms.

items.title: CERC proposes Grid India as market coupling operator
items.summary: In a bid to revolutionize electricity trading in India, the National Electricity Regulatory Authority is unveiling proposed regulations that will allow the Grid Controller of India Ltd to implement market coupling. This strategic initiative aims to standardize pricing across various electricity markets, enhancing transparency and efficiency.

items.title: Drug regulator transfers 95 officials in mass reshuffle
items.summary: The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation has moved about 95 officials across India. This reshuffle aims to improve compliance and lessen the impact of long-serving officers on the pharmaceutical sector. The move follows a structured transfer policy.

items.title: SC asks NCLT to expedite call on Supertech oversight panel
items.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the National Company Law Tribunal to quickly decide if a court-appointed committee is needed for Supertech's 30 stalled projects. This comes after the interim resolution professional was suspended. Homebuyers are concerned about project completion. The tribunal will consider if the committee is necessary for all projects or just those taken up by NBCC.

items.title: Trump threatens Iran again as ceasefire deadline looms, U.S. gears up for peace talks
items.summary: Trump reportedly said Sunday that if Iran doesn't sign a deal, "the whole country is going to get blown up," with its bridges and power plants being targeted.

items.title: UAE hotels find peace in war, go for renovations and upgrades
items.summary: Premium hotels in Dubai are undertaking renovations. This is happening due to a slowdown in tourist numbers. Some hotels will close for up to 18 months. Others are pausing restaurant operations. Industry experts expect more luxury properties to follow. This strategy aims to minimize revenue disruption. It also prepares for future demand upcycles.

items.title: SC asks NCLAT to take decision on 14 housing projects of debt-ridden Supertech
items.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the NCLAT to quickly decide on merging Supertech's remaining 14 housing projects. This move aims to ensure project completion and resolve homebuyers' long-standing issues. The NCLAT will hear all stakeholders, including land agencies and farmers. This follows a previous order for NBCC to complete 16 other Supertech projects.

items.title: Telcos test waters with smaller tariff hikes before big one
items.summary: Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea and Reliance Jio are changing their prepaid plans. Airtel has increased prices and added streaming subscriptions. Vodafone Idea is restructuring plans, especially for 5G. Jio reduced validity on a basic plan but also added OTT benefits. These moves aim to boost revenue without direct tariff hikes.

items.title: Hormuz disruptions hit China’s Christmas capital — and holiday spending
items.summary: Manufacturers told CNBC that shoppers will likely have to pay higher prices to match rising costs of plastic and other materials.

items.title: Room with a view! More branded hotels check into state capitals
items.summary: Branded hotels are set to expand across India's state capitals. Patna alone will see its branded hotel pipeline grow to eleven. Across the country, an estimated 60,253 new hotel rooms are expected in the next five years. Cities like Lucknow, Kolkata, Shimla, and Raipur are also anticipating new hotel developments.

items.title: Trump’s call to deregulate psychedelics boosts drugmakers’ shares
items.summary: Stock in UK-based Compass Pathways jumps to highest level in two years following president’s executive order

items.title: Rick Perry’s Fermi Is Undermining the AI Energy Thesis
items.summary: The Fermi Paradox asks why, given the vastness of the universe, there is no hard evidence of aliens. The Fermi Inc. paradox asks why, given seemingly insatiable demand from hyperscalers for electricity, former Energy Secretary Rick Perry’s AI-energy firm hasn’t obtained a hard power contract from said hyperscalers.

items.title: Trump tariff refunds begin but consumers likely to miss out
items.summary: Businesses can apply online through a portal for refunds expected to total $160bn.

items.title: India's premium liquor boom defies global slowdown
items.summary: India's demand for premium liquor is surging. This trend defies global patterns where consumers are buying less expensive options. Despite a worldwide decline in alcohol sales volume and value, India saw growth. This indicates a strong consumer preference for prestige whiskies and premium white spirits.

items.title: Global strategic oil inventory totaled 2.5 billion barrels at end of 2025, EIA says
items.summary: Global strategic oil reserves reached 2.5 billion barrels by year-end. China, the United States, and Japan hold the largest stockpiles. The US is releasing oil to lower prices. Other nations also maintain significant reserves. China notably increased its reserves in recent years.

items.title: Bain Capital sole contender for Vitabiotics buy
items.summary: Bain Capital has emerged as the frontrunner to acquire Vitabiotics, as rival bidders TPG Capital and EQT exit the process, potentially lowering the company’s expected £900 million valuation. Founded in 1971, Vitabiotics is the UK’s largest multivitamin firm and operates across key markets including India and China. Its India arm contributes about 20% of total revenue.

items.title: Chair nominee Kevin Warsh says Fed must ‘stay in its lane’ to maintain independence
items.summary: Warsh also expressed firm commitment to fighting inflation with only one mention of the labor market.

items.title: RBI plans to remain nimble to prevent persistent supply shock amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed global challenges at Princeton University, stressing upon agile monetary policy to counter supply shocks from West Asia. The focus is on preventing inflation from becoming persistent. Prudent fiscal management and domestic production also play key roles, he said.

items.title: Deal activity value jumps 18 pc to USD 123.8 bn in 2025; volumes drop marginally
items.summary: India's deal activity saw a significant rise in 2025. Deal values increased by 18 percent to USD 123.8 billion. This growth occurred even as the number of transactions decreased. Investors focused on larger, strategic deals in infrastructure and financial services. Cross-border deals also saw a substantial value increase. This indicates continued investor confidence in the Indian market.

items.title: Suzlon enters into initial pact with Korean firm GS E&C for RE biz in India
items.summary: Suzlon Energy and Korean firm GS E&C have signed an initial pact to partner in India's renewable energy sector, focusing on project development and solution optimization. Additionally, GS E&C will cooperate with Arie Energy LLP on wind farm repowering projects. AFINIT and Yes Bank also inked an MoU to enhance financial inclusion in India.

items.title: US oil refiners reap windfall from Iran war
items.summary: Soaring fuel prices and access to cheap North American crude oil put American refiners in pole position

items.title: KTM 350cc Duke and Adventure launched in India: Price, specs, and what's different
items.summary: KTM has launched new 350cc variants of the 390 Duke and 390 Adventure in India, leveraging a lower GST slab to make its performance bikes more accessible. Priced significantly lower than their 399cc counterparts, these models offer a compelling entry point into KTM's aspirational lineup.

items.title: Welspun Enterprises picks up 4.5 lakh sq ft office space in Pune’s Hinjewadi
items.summary: Welspun Enterprises has secured a significant office space deal in Pune's Hinjewadi IT hub. The company leased over 4.5 lakh square feet of office space. This transaction marks one of the larger office deals in Pune this year. The lease agreement is for five years. Hinjewadi continues to be a prime location for businesses seeking expansion.

items.title: Trump’s social media posts have transformed oil trading, says Citadel
items.summary: Hedge fund’s commodities head says traders are struggling to adjust to volatility sparked by president’s frequent messages

items.title: CBI arrests two RCOM senior officials in bank 'fraud' case
items.summary: The two executives were important functionaries of the Reliance Communications group, managing corporate finance, banking operations, payments/utilisation of funds, etc.

items.title: Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for $250 million over alcohol abuse claims
items.summary: The Atlantic article said FBI Director Kash Patel "has alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences."

items.title: Another flight leaves passengers behind due to border delays
items.summary: New European border rules have caused delays at airports across the continent, affecting flights.

items.title: Phase-I of bilateral trade agreement with US almost ready: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Trade conversations are heating up in Washington as India and the US engage in crucial discussions aimed at enhancing economic ties. At the forefront are negotiations on preferential market access that could allow for a more favorable entry of Indian products into the American market.

items.title: Global wind installations rise 40pc in 2025, industry charts way out of energy crisis: Report
items.summary: The Global Wind Energy Council's (GWEC) 2026 Report, published at a time of supply shocks and fast-rising prices in international oil and gas markets, shows how a resilient wind industry continues to grow at pace as a lynchpin of the energy transition, a statement said.

items.title: India, South Korea working to double bilateral trade by 2030: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Both countries have been tasked by their leadership to raise two-way trade from the current $27 billion to $54 billion by 2030, says commerce minister Piyush Goyal

items.title: Electricity likely to cost more in Delhi; APTEL orders to start liquidation of discom dues in 3 weeks
items.summary: Delhi's electricity bills are set to increase. The Appellate Tribunal for Electricity has ordered the Delhi power regulator to start recovering over Rs 38,500 crore owed by electricity distribution companies. This move comes after the tribunal rejected a request for a CAG audit, directing an independent chartered accountant instead. The process must begin within three weeks, impacting city residents.

items.title: India doubles wheat export quota to 5 million tonnes
items.summary: The move comes after a comprehensive review of current production, stock availability, and price trends

items.title: TVS Motor Co enters Zambia, lines up 8 products
items.summary: TVS Motor Company has officially entered the Zambian market, partnering with Zamoto Manufacturing Ltd as its distributor. This strategic move aims to expand TVS's African presence by offering a diverse range of eight products, catering to various mobility needs in the rapidly urbanizing country. The company is introducing models for taxi, commercial, personal, and premium segments to meet local demands.

items.title: India's core sector contracts 0.4% in March, hurt by West Asia conflict
items.summary: The index of eight core industries, which tracks coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement and electricity, accounts for 40% of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), a broader measure of factory output in the economy.

items.title: Investors are misreading news about the Iran war, analysts say as markets whipsaw
items.summary: Monday's declines put the brakes on another rapid recovery in global stock indexes akin to last year's 'liberation day' bounce.

items.title: Jack Daniel's owner eyeing shake-up? Decades-old distiller favours Pernod amid struggling US spirits market - Report
items.summary: With the potential tie-up, Brown-Forman could reportedly have a better route to market Jack Daniel’s in China and India – where demand for whiskey is rising, given Pernod's wide distribution network

items.title: IFSCA grants registration to first foreign family office in GIFT City under new fund framework
items.summary: The registration relates to a UK-based family office structure and has been granted to Poornam Asset Management (IFSC) Pvt Ltd, which is acting as the IFSC-based fund management platform

items.title: Govt reduces jute stock limits to zero
items.summary: The government has set raw jute stock limits to zero for traders and balers. This move aims to ensure fair distribution and prevent hoarding. Jute mills and processing units can hold stocks for 45 days. These measures intend to stabilize jute supply and support farmers, manufacturers, and consumers. Entities must declare stock positions fortnightly.

items.title: European stocks slide as Gulf tanker attacks threaten fragile ceasefire
items.summary: European stocks traded lower on Monday as traders assess a re-acceleration in U.S.-Iran tensions.

items.title: Q4 Results Highlights: BoM & Groww Q4 profit soar, Navkar Corporation turns profitable, PNB Housing, Nelco, Indosolar, Indbank Merchant to announce Q4, ICICI & HDFC Bank stocks end flat, Yes Bank shares down
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, April 20, 2026, Highlights: Catch updates

items.title: Table Space adds 4.2 lakh sq ft in Maharashtra as GCC demand drives managed office boom
items.summary: Table Space has significantly expanded its managed office space in Maharashtra. The company added 425,000 square feet across Pune and Navi Mumbai. This expansion boosts their regional presence to 2.6 million square feet. Pune locations include Wakad, Yerwada and Koregaon Park.

items.title: Govt doubles down on wheat exports, clears extra 2.5 million tonnes
items.summary: The decision is expected to enhance market liquidity, facilitate efficient stock management, and prevent distress sales during the peak arrival season, the government said on Monday.

items.title: 'Resumption of hostilities': Seized ship, vessel attacks push U.S.-Iran ceasefire toward brink
items.summary: A U.S. seizure of an Iranian cargo ship and reports of vessels coming under fire in the Gulf have pushed a fragile ceasefire with Tehran to the brink.

items.title: Kothari Industrial Corporation shares now listed on the NSE as well
items.summary: The company’s shares are already listed on the BSE

items.title: Karnataka liquor taxation: Distillers to meet CM, seek review of new alcobev tax structure
items.summary: Alcoholic beverage manufacturers in Bengaluru are seeking a review of proposed excise duty rules, fearing a European model's negative impact. The state government plans to introduce an alcohol-in-beverage (AIB) based excise duty structure, reducing pricing slabs. Distillers are concerned about duty hikes in lower slabs and hope for moderation.

items.title: Kevin Warsh would be the first tech bro Fed chair. How Silicon Valley shaped him
items.summary: The nominee to lead the Fed is an AI optimist who counts tech titans Peter Thiel and Marc Andreessen among his friends.

items.title: The luxury of hindsight, the urgency of foresight: What India can still get right
items.summary: Iran war: Recent West Asian events highlight India's vulnerabilities in energy, trade and finance. Foresight was dismissed, but now hindsight offers lessons. India must prepare for future disruptions by institutionalising scenario planning and risk assessment. This proactive approach is crucial for navigating global pressures and maintaining policy space.

items.title: Postal Department revenue jumps 16% YoY to ₹15,296 crore, says Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia — Check details
items.summary: The Department of Post has registered an annual revenue of ₹15,296 crore for FY26, up 16% YoY, Union Communications Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said today on 20 April, calling it a “historic” year.

items.title: IEX shares tumble 8% after CERC draft on market coupling raises concerns
items.summary: The decline followed the release of a draft proposal by the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission on electricity price discovery, which introduces market coupling norms that could significantly alter the dynamics of power exchanges in India

items.title: Eli Lilly agrees to acquire cancer drug maker Kelonia in deal worth up to $7 billion
items.summary: Kelonia is developing technology to reprogram patients' T-cells inside the body so those cells can attack cancer, called in vivo CAR-T.

items.title: Hyundai, TVS sign joint development pact for commercialisation of electric 3-wheeler in India
items.summary: Hyundai Motor and TVS Motor are joining forces. They will develop new electric three-wheelers for India. This partnership aims to improve last-mile transport. Hyundai will lead design and technology. TVS will bring its electric platform and local market knowledge. Local manufacturing will be a key focus. This will create jobs and reduce costs. The first vehicle is expected soon.

items.title: Mumbai airport: Flight operations to remain suspended for 6 hours on May 7 for runway maintenance works
items.summary: Mumbai airport will halt flights for six hours on May 7. Both runways will be closed from 11 am to 5 pm for essential pre-monsoon maintenance. This annual work ensures the safety of landings and take-offs during the monsoon season. Airlines have adjusted schedules in advance. The airport operator stated the maintenance is crucial for airside infrastructure.

items.title: Testing the waters? Why Airtel is raising the tariff of a high-value prepaid plan
items.summary: The telecom major’s latest price tweak may be a calculated gamble to gauge subscriber ‘stickiness’ before broader, industry-wide hikes, experts told Mint.

items.title: Widening CAD a concern, says Niti Report
items.summary: In its quarterly Trade Watch Report for the quarter to December 2026, released on Monday, it said the instability in West Asia has slowed the India-Gulf Cooperation Council Free Trade Agreement (FTA), affecting trade diversification and market access. "FTAs are not a one way street, nor should they be, which is to say that in the way that we are seeing them as a tool for market access, others are seeing it as a tool for market access too," said NITI Aayog vice chairman Suman Bery.

items.title: Health insurance coverage rises, higher in rural areas: Survey
items.summary: Health insurance coverage in India has seen a major jump. Rural areas now have more people insured than urban centers. Government schemes are driving this growth. Out-of-pocket hospital expenses have also been reported. Morbidity levels have risen, with infections being the main reason for hospitalisation. Institutional deliveries are now almost universal across the country.

items.title: American Airlines falls after company dismisses talk of United megamerger
items.summary: American Airlines stock fell after the company dismissed talk of a potential merger with United, citing antitrust concerns and potential harm to competition.

items.title: Exporters’ support lift orthodox leaf prices in Coonoor tea auctions
items.summary: Global Tea Auctioneers said the sales percentage in leaf grades was 84% out of the offered quantity of 14,08,201 kg, while dust grades maintained 89% sales out of the offered quantity of 4,74,137 kg.

items.title: Fish waste finds new life as high-value biomedical material
items.summary: Scientists at ICAR-Central Institute of Fisheries Technology (ICAR-CIFT), Kochi, have developed a patented technology that converts fish scale waste into nanofibre-based graft materials capable of accelerating bone and dental healing.

items.title: Core sector output fell 0.4% in March, lowest in 19 months
items.summary: India’s core sector output contracted 0.4% year-on-year in March, marking its lowest level in 19 months, driven by a sharp decline in fertiliser production amid the West Asia conflict and weaker performance across energy-related industries, according to official data released on Monday.

items.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
items.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

items.title: Broker’s call: Angel One (Add)
items.summary: JM Financial

items.title: Washington-backed rare earth group to buy Brazilian miner for $2.8bn
items.summary: USA Rare Earth’s deal for Serra Verde highlights America’s push to secure access to critical minerals

items.title: RBI draft for upper layer non-banks affects CICs disproportionately, raises compliances costs
items.summary: New Reserve Bank draft on upper layer non-bank finance companies disproportionately impacts core investment companies by increasing compliance costs. Mandatory listing requirements could prove onerous for many CICs, particularly those not seeking public market access. The AUM-based approach may present challenges for these entities, especially regarding listing and governance.

items.title: MCX to launch coal exchange
items.summary: The exchange is aimed at developing a regulated, transparent, technology-driven market platform for buying and selling coal

items.title: Govt reviews rural schemes, supply chain woes to protect beneficiaries from Iran war impact
items.summary: The Rural Development Ministry is reviewing its key schemes in response to the West Asia conflict's potential impact on global supply chains and prices. The ministry is ensuring uninterrupted employment and timely fund flow for beneficiaries. Measures are being taken to protect rural housing and road projects. This includes timely fund releases and material bank creation.

items.title: Broker’s call: Sagility (Buy)
items.summary: Elara Securities

items.title: Vedanta fixes May 1 as record date for business split
Certus Capital to invest ₹2000 cr in housing, mixed-use projects in FY27
items.summary: The push comes amid a surge in capital flows into Indian real estate, with investments hitting a record $14.3 billion in 2025, up 25% year-on-year

items.title: Radisson Hotel Group expects to create 65,000-80,000 job opportunities in India by 2030 with 500 properties
items.summary: Radisson Hotel Group is embarking on an ambitious journey to enhance its footprint in India, targeting the launch of 500 hotels by 2030. This initiative is projected to create up to 80,000 jobs, focusing on upscale and mid-scale offerings in diverse city environments. Committed to local community enrichment, the group is also prioritizing skill development to foster career growth.

items.title: IIT Kharagpur launches Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence AI centre to transform mining operations across India
items.summary: IIT Kharagpur has launched the Vikram Sodhi Centre of Excellence for AI-Enabled Geological & Mining Systems, funded by a Rs 15 crore commitment. This new center aims to revolutionize India's mining sector by integrating AI across operations, from exploration to ESG analytics, transforming data-rich but information-poor environments into efficient, data-driven systems.

items.title: RBI allows NDF contracts to clients, rebooking of related party trades
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has made a pivotal move by retracting its earlier April 1 directive, which had restricted banks from offering non-deliverable forward contracts and barred the rebooking of cancelled foreign exchange derivative contracts. This reversal allows banks to enjoy increased operational flexibility, fostering a more fluid trading environment.

items.title: FY27 capex growth of states pegged at 8–10%: report
items.summary: State capital spending growth is set to slow in FY27. This moderation follows a strong FY26. Rising revenue expenses and slower revenue growth are key factors. Geopolitical issues in West Asia could further impact state finances. Fiscal discipline will be crucial for states balancing welfare and investment needs. Top states continue to prioritize infrastructure development.

items.title: USA Rare Earth to buy Brazil's Serra Verde for $2.8 billion to build supply outside Asia
items.summary: USA Rare Earth on Monday announced plans to buy Brazilian rare earths miner Serra Verde in a deal worth $2.8 billion in cash and shares.

items.title: Gold falls on inflation fears, firmer dollar amid renewed US-Iran tensions
items.summary: Gold hits lowest level in a week

items.title: India’s core sector growth contracts 0.4% in March 2026 to lowest level since August 2024
items.summary: March 2026 brought unexpected challenges for India's foundational industries, as the Index of Eight Core Industries reported a contraction of 0.4 percent. The sharp declines in essential sectors such as fertiliser, crude oil, coal, and electricity contributed significantly to this downturn, reversing the positive momentum that had been evident just a month prior.

items.title: GIFT City grants first family office permit
items.summary: Permit granted to Poornam Asset Management IFSC ‌Pvt Ltd

items.title: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry
items.summary: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry

items.title: India growth resilient, markets near correction: HDFC Securities
items.summary: India's economy is set for robust growth. Real GDP is projected at 6.5 percent for FY26-FY27. Nominal GDP will expand between 10 to 11 percent. Inflation is expected to ease to 4.5 percent. Fiscal deficit targets are set at 4.3 percent. Retail investor participation remains strong. Markets may be nearing the bottom of the current correction cycle.

items.title: How the Iran war affects your money and bills
items.summary: The conflict in the Middle East has increased pressure on the cost of petrol, household energy bills and even food.

items.title: JAL insolvency case: Lenders flag ‘leak’ that may have aided Vedanta bid
items.summary: Following the alleged leak, Vedanta submitted a revised bid with improved net present value and equity infusion, which was rejected for missing the deadline.

items.title: Vedanta vs Adani: Jaiprakash case may test limits of ‘commercial wisdom’
items.summary: Lawyers say the Anil Agarwal-led Vedanta's challenge is serious and, if successful, could force lenders to better explain their decisions, especially when choosing a lower-value plan based on factors like upfront cash and faster payments.

items.title: What's happening to UK petrol and diesel prices?
items.summary: UK petrol and diesel prices have started to fall after 46 consecutive days of rises at the pump.

items.title: UniCredit boss plots Commerzbank shake-up as lender continues its takeover pursuit of German rival
items.summary: UniCredit's CEO said the combination would deliver "significant cross-border value" and investment "firepower".

items.title: Alivaa Hotels bets on unbranded market for growth to 80 properties by FY27; seeks to raise $4 million
items.summary: Alivaa Hotels is capitalizing on India's unbranded hotel market by franchising small properties. With a unique performance-based revenue model, the company aims for 80 operational hotels by the end of the fiscal year.

items.title: China calls for ‘concerted’ industry efforts to tackle excess solar production
items.summary: China is the undisputed global leader in solar energy, making more than 80% of the world's solar panel components.

items.title: India, South Korea to upgrade trade pact, deepen tech and supply chain ties
items.summary: India and South Korea will resume talks to upgrade their economic partnership agreement. The goal is to increase bilateral trade to 50 billion dollars by 2030. Both nations will also enhance cooperation in critical technologies and supply chains. An economic security dialogue will be established. Discussions will cover shipbuilding, steel, and sustainability. Industrial collaboration will expand through new agreements.

items.title: Dharavi project begins; 11,000 rehab homes being constructed on railway land in Matunga
UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank inevitable without change of course
items.summary: BRIEF-UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank 'inevitabl:BRIEF-UniCredit CEO says restructuring at Commerzbank 'inevitable' without change of course

items.title: UP allows wheat sale at MSP without online registration
items.summary: Uttar Pradesh farmers can now sell wheat at government procurement centers without prior online registration. This decision comes after many farmers faced difficulties registering and were forced to sell to middlemen. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has ordered immediate implementation. Farmers can now sell their wheat as they did in previous years, ensuring fair prices for their produce.

items.title: Yes, tokens do have real uses in finance
items.summary: Tokenisation is taking hold in areas including money, bonds, gold, funds, real estate and private equity

items.title: Tata Trusts move to allow non-Zoroastrians won’t validate current trustees: Mehli Mistry
items.summary: Mistry also said allowing non-Zoroastrians as trustees at Bai Hirabai trust cannot apply retrospectively.

items.title: The looming battle over the Fed’s balance sheet
items.summary: What will Warsh choose?

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Ship struck and Strait shut
items.summary: A dramatic escalation in tension over the Strait of Hormuz drives oil prices and risk assets higher in early trade.

items.title: Japan's Mitsubishi Heavy Industries sees shares jump nearly 4% on first ever warship export deal
items.summary: This will be Japan's first ever warship export project, and the first ship is scheduled to be delivered to the Royal Australian Navy in 2029.

items.title: US stablecoins pose danger to emerging markets, say central bankers
items.summary: Growth of dollar-pegged crypto assets under Donald Trump risks accelerating dollarisation and enabling criminal misuse

items.summary: Plus, RIP Mark Mobius, BlackRock cools on Europe and Jasper Johns in Bilbao

items.title: Private credit gets the once-over
items.summary: Do you want the bad news or the bad news?

items.title: South Korea's Lee to seek big boost in economic ties in summit with PM Modi
items.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is in New Delhi for talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The visit aims to significantly increase economic ties, focusing on shipbuilding. Both nations plan to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Cooperation may also expand into finance, artificial intelligence, and defense. India's role in global production is highlighted.

items.title: Inflation anxiety may have made a comeback, especially for low-income groups
items.summary: The latest survey by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) reveals growing inflation anxiety, with households’ perceptions and expectations of inflation rising. These perceptions are usually influenced by the food and fuel price outlook.

items.title: Risk Management: Financial Institutions
items.summary: The soaring level of US government debt; how AI is increasing the speed and depth of cyber attacks; and geopolitical shocks have highlighted the need for diversity in cloud providers

items.title: Oil prices set for more turbulence in months ahead, warns Gunvor chief
items.summary: World’s fourth-largest independent crude trader says markets may be ‘very choppy’ between April and June

items.title: How the Iran war could hurt Wall Street
items.summary: The big unknown is how the conflict could alter the Gulf’s investment priorities in the next year or two

items.title: Germany to begin privatisation of seized Gazprom division
items.summary: Head of company now known as Sefe says capital increase could raise up to €2bn

items.title: A retro rally is coming from e-merging markets
items.summary: For now South Korea and Taiwan are the biggest beneficiaries of the AI wave

items.title: Life and annuity industry is moving into riskier assets
items.summary: Private capital groups have come to control hundreds of billions of dollars of retirement savings

items.title: Transatlantic divide widens over investment approaches to ESG
items.summary: In the US, political pressure has pushed sustainable investing further down the priority list

items.title: India’s affluent consumers spend more, but retail’s share is shrinking
items.summary: Affluent Indians are shifting spending toward experiences, shrinking retail’s share even as premium purchases rise. Retailers are adding immersive formats to keep up.

items.title: Ads on tap: Why Jaquar still values old-school marketing in the age of AI
items.summary: While the bath fittings and lighting giant is leveraging AI for insights and footfall, it remains wary of ‘plastic’ generative visuals, choosing instead to double down on human-led branding and its legacy dealer network.

items.title: Tata Trusts affirms its faith in the CEO; it will review restrictive clauses that limit eligibility in the smaller trust
items.summary: After a meeting of the Bai Hirabai Jamsetji Tata Navsari Charitable board on Friday, Tata Trusts said its ethos is inclusive, secular, all-encompassing, and focused on philanthropy and national service.

items.title: Cognizant's board will oversee its AI usage
items.summary: Cognizant's board of directors will monitor the use of AI tools and their financial impact on the company.

items.title: Why your recycled clothes could end up in this South American desert
items.summary: Old garments from around the world are being discarded in the South American country.

items.title: U.S. struck, seized Iranian-flagged ship Touska in Gulf of Oman, Trump says
items.summary: The Iran-flagged Touska was on the U.S. Treasury's sanctions list because of a history of illegal activity, Trump said.

items.title: Iran rebuffs Trump's plan for new round of peace talks, state media reports
items.summary: Iran's top negotiator says his government has "no trust" in its enemies, after Trump says the U.S. won't be blackmailed.

items.title: Aer Lingus cancels some flights from summer schedule
items.summary: The airline said the "vast majority of customers" are being accommodated on same-day services.

items.title: Merz Sets Crisis Talks to Tackle Energy Price Impact on Germany
items.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he plans to convene Germany’s national security council to discuss the global energy crisis, signaling increased concern about its impact on Europe’s biggest economy.

items.title: Finance ministry may review MOOWR benefits for battery storage imports
items.summary: The Finance Ministry is set to discuss customs warehousing benefits for imported battery storage systems. Industry groups have raised concerns about the MOOWR scheme creating an uneven playing field. Consultations with power and renewable energy ministries are planned. The government is examining potential misuse and policy gaps. A decision will align with India's energy security goals.

items.title: Gig workers social security scheme may have multiple fund managers
items.summary: The labour ministry of India is charting a new course by investigating a multi-manager framework for its latest social security initiative aimed at gig and platform workers. The EPFO is likely to manage the provident fund components, while various fund managers will look after pension allocations.

items.title: ‘Parsi-only’ rule in focus: Bai Hirabai Trust to move for change in ‘restrictive’ trustee clauses - Tata Trusts
items.summary: In a meeting on 17 April, the trustees reviewed the activities of the Bai Hirabai Trust and deliberated on the recent statements appearing in sections of the press relating to the trust

items.title: Modernizing the ledger: Inside the quest to solidify India’s economic data
items.summary: India’s GDP workings have been beset by critiques over the past decade. But new revisions aim to accurately capture the resilience of the country’s formalizing economy.

items.title: China’s Orient Securities Deal to Create $86 Billion Brokerage
items.summary: Two Shanghai government‑backed brokerages plan to merge in a deal that will create a firm with around $86 billion in assets, underscoring China’s push to consolidate the securities industry as it seeks to build world‑class investment banks.

items.title: Multiple states to borrow Rs 16,900 crore through RBI SGS auction on April 21
items.summary: Multiple state governments are set to raise a combined Rs 16,900 crore through a revised auction of State Government Securities on April 21. The Reserve Bank of India will conduct the auction on its E-Kuber platform, featuring fresh issuances and re-issues with varying tenors. Andhra Pradesh, Punjab, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, and Telangana are among the participating states.

items.title: CII suggests GIS-enabled land bank, uniform stamp duty to speed up acquisition
items.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has proposed a unified national industrial land bank with a GIS-enabled system to streamline land acquisition for manufacturing. The proposal includes standardized social impact assessments, district-level acquisition cells, and a dispute registry to reduce timelines and enhance transparency.

items.title: Govt may hike FDI limit in pension sector; Bill likely in Monsoon Session
items.summary: The government is poised to significantly boost foreign direct investment in the pension sector, potentially raising the limit to 100%. A bill to this effect is anticipated in upcoming parliamentary sessions. This move mirrors the insurance sector's recent FDI liberalization. Additionally, proposals include separating the NPS Trust from the PFRDA, aiming for independent management.

items.title: AI-proof your future with these 3 skills — Father of AGI says ‘human jobs will become obsolete’
items.summary: Ben Goertzel said a few roles, like educators and teachers, will remain intact – unaffected by the AGI because a human touch remains critical.

items.title: India resilient amid oil shock, may grow 6.8%-7.1% despite global headwinds: SBI Research
items.summary: India faces global challenges from a strong position. Economic growth is projected between 6.8% and 7.1% for FY27. The nation's banking sector is robust. Opportunities arise for GIFT City and Indian airports. The Reserve Bank of India is likely to maintain its current monetary policy stance. India's resilience is evident amidst regional conflict and global uncertainties.
